description_long: This is a heavy-duty sledgehammer with a robust, rectangular metal head that shows significant red paint chipping and surface wear, revealing a darker, possibly rusted base metal beneath. The head is thick and blocky, with flat striking faces on both ends, designed for delivering powerful blows. The handle is straight and appears to be made of wood, wrapped with a dark, textured grip that extends most of its length, likely for improved handling and shock absorption. The end of the handle is rounded and also shows signs of wear, with some of the grip material appearing frayed or abraded. The overall appearance suggests extensive use, with visible scuffs, scratches, and discoloration, especially on the head. This tool is typical of those found in urban construction sites, garages, or maintenance areas.
description: A well-worn sledgehammer with a chipped red metal head and a straight, dark-gripped wooden handle, showing heavy use and surface wear.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad, flat face of the sledgehammer head with extensive red paint chipping and a centered handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the rectangular thickness of the head and the length of the straight, dark-wrapped handle.
description_view_2: Rear view focuses on the end of the handle, revealing its rounded, textured grip and more wear on the handle's surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the other flat face of the head and the full length of the handle, emphasizing the chipped paint and scuffed surfaces.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle wrapped with a dark, textured, rubberized grip for enhanced hold and shock absorption.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular sledgehammer head made of steel, with flat striking faces on both ends and no claws or peens.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Extensive red paint chipping, surface scratches, scuffs, and some rust or discoloration on the metal head; fraying and abrasion on the handle grip.
